claims.claim_text: On a single GPU, parameter-efficient methods are usually the practical path because they reduce trainable parameters and memory pressure relative to full fine-tuning.
claims.span_hint: section: Why PEFT Is the Default Here (lines 130-136)
claims.rationale: The LoRA paper and Hugging Face PEFT LoRA guide explicitly describe reduced trainable-parameter count, lower memory use, and practical LoRA-based fine-tuning workflows.

claims.claim_text: Quantization can make local single-GPU fine-tuning feasible, but it changes performance and compatibility expectations.
claims.rationale: QLoRA and the Transformers bitsandbytes guide directly cover 4-bit constrained-VRAM fine-tuning, hardware compatibility constraints, and quantization-related quality/performance considerations.

further_reading.title: QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized LLMs
further_reading.why_relevant: Best primary source for what makes realistic single-GPU fine-tuning possible under tight VRAM budgets.

further_reading.title: PEFT LoRA Developer Guide
further_reading.why_relevant: Covers the practical LoRA configuration choices and adapter mechanics learners will use in real local tuning workflows.

further_reading.title: Transformers bitsandbytes Quantization Guide
further_reading.why_relevant: Useful for the concrete 4-bit and nested-quantization trade-offs that shape single-GPU local experiments.

notes: This module is mostly pedagogical. Most remaining sentences are workflow advice, framing, or examples rather than hard factual claims that need inline citations.
